label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what can be inferred from the passage about the new Presidential 1 coins. Let me go through each option. First, the passage mentions that the new Presidential 1 coins are the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. So, if the new coin is gold-colored and same as the Sacagawea, then option C, which says the new coin takes on a white color, is definitely wrong. So C is out. Option D says the Sacagawea Golden Dollar is bigger. But the passage says the new coin is the same size as it, so D is incorrect. So D is out. Option B: the new coin can buy more than the old coin. The passage doesn't mention anything about purchasing power or value. It just says they're trading old dollar bills for the new coin. The value of the coin itself is a dollar, same as the bill. So B isn't supported. Option A: there are no words on the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. The passage says that the new coins have inscriptions on the edge for the first time since 1930s. The Sacagawea Golden Dollar doesn't have this because the new coins are different in that aspect. So does that mean the Sacagawea doesn't have edge inscriptions? If the new coins
